steps.title: Calculate the original mean
steps.content:
Sum the five egg counts from the table:
$$149 + 144 + 148 + 136 + 139 = 716$$
Divide by 5 to find the original mean:
$$\text{Original mean} = \frac{716}{5} = 143.2$$

steps.title: Calculate the new mean and compare
steps.content: Adding the sixth nest with 121 eggs: sum $= 716 + 121 = 837$. New mean $= \frac{837}{6} = 139.5$. Since $143.2 > 139.5$, the original mean is greater.

steps.title: Check all choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'The mean of the original data set is greater than the mean of the new data set.': Original mean $143.2 >$ new mean $139.5$ ✓
  • Choice B — 'The mean of the original data set is less than the mean of the new data set.': $143.2 \lt 139.5$ is false ✗
  • Choice C — 'The means of both data sets are equal.': $143.2 \ne 139.5$ ✗
  • Choice D — 'There is not enough information to compare the means.': All values are given so a direct comparison is possible ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
